options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: assert-allowed
commands.description:
Finds and reads all project files within the target directory,
then extracts the package information to check the license
types. Fails on any packages that has a not approved license

options.name: --ignored-packages
options.description:
The name of packages that should be excluded from the
license extraction. No packages are excluded by
default. To ignore multiple packages, specify the flag
multiple times, e.g.: -ip=Package1 -ip=Package2

options.name: --input-path
options.description:
The path to the dictionary where the input projects
are located. All contained csproj files will be
recursively enumerated. The lockfile will be saved to
this location. This will be set as the working
directory for the entire application - all other paths
will be relative to this folder. Defaults to the
dictionary where the tool was started from.

options.name: --cache-path
options.description:
The path to the dictionary where the application cache
folder can be created. Defaults to the local
application data folder.

options.name: --package-path
options.description:
The path to the dictionary where the project NuGet
packages are stored. Usually configured as
globalPackagesFolder in a nuget.config file. Has no
default value. The tool can automatically discover
some of the local NuGet package caches, so only
specify this value if the tool won't work otherwise.

options.name: --project-filters
options.description:
Regex filters for excluding projects from processing.
Checks the relative project path, not just the .csproj
file name. Case insensitive. This filter is only
applied when recursively looking for projects within
the working directory. If a non-ignored project
references an ignored project, the ignored project
will still be processed. Defaults to [(.*)Test(.*)]

options.name: --print-table
options.description:
Whether the write the package table to the standard
output. Defaults to false. To enable, specify the flag
without any values, e.g.: -pt

options.name: --log-level
options.description:
Sets the logging level. Accepted values are: Trace,
Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Defaults to Information.

options.name: --config-path
options.description:
The path to the configuration file, relative to the
input path (specified by command line parameter).
Defaults to "./licensetool-config.json"

options.name: --no-restore
options.description:
Whether the skip restoring NuGet packages. NuGet
package restore is a mandatory pre-requisite, but it
can be performed manually beforehand. Defaults to
false, that is, restore is performed by this
application. To skip restore, specify the flag without
any values, e.g.: --no-restore

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: configure
commands.description:
Creates an example configuration file in the current working
directory. That file then can be manually edited and used with
the other commands. When a config file is found, the tool
automatically reads and parses it, overwriting all other CLI
parameters. The log level can only be configured using the
command line parameter.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: update
commands.description:
Finds and reads all project files within the target directory,
then extracts the package information into a package lock
file. Then, extracts all package licenses into the target
directory, based on the lock file. Fails when the license of
any package cannot be determined, cannot be accepted, or the
license file does not exists.

options.name: --license-path
options.description:
The path of the directory where the license files
should be extracted. Relative to the input directory
path. Defaults to "licenses".

options.name: --ignored-packages
options.description:
The name of packages that should be excluded from the
license extraction. No packages are excluded by
default. To ignore multiple packages, specify the flag
multiple times, e.g.: -ip=Package1 -ip=Package2

will be relative to this folder. Defaults to the
dictionary where the tool was started from.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: validate
commands.description:
Reads all package information in the target directory, and
compares that against the existing package lock file. Fails on
unexpected projects, packages and package versions.

options.name: --input-path
options.description:

located. All contained csproj files will be recursively

relative to this folder. Defaults to the dictionary
where the tool was started from.

commands.name: version
commands.description:
Writes the tool version number to the standard output.
Run 'mirango-licensetool [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.
1.5.6
